dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Real-time statistics from your identity platform.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your account preferences and settings
settings.twoFactorHelper: Add an extra layer of security to your account
settings.darkModeHelper: Use dark theme for reduced eye strain
settings.compactViewHelper: Show more content with reduced spacing
settings.securityWarning: You are about to change security settings. This may affect your current session. Continue?
settings.passwordHelper: Min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special character
settings.otpVerificationHelper: Send and verify one-time passwords via email or SMS
settings.stepUpAuthHelper: Register a device for step-up authentication with challenge-response

otp.title: OTP Management
otp.description: Send and verify one-time passwords to confirm your email or phone number.
otp.sendEmailPrompt: Send a one-time verification code to your registered email address.
otp.sendSmsPrompt: Send a one-time verification code to your registered phone number.
otp.enterCode: Enter the verification code you received:
otp.verifyFailed: OTP verification failed. Please check the code and try again.

stepUp.title: Step-Up Device Registration
stepUp.description: Register a device for step-up authentication using ECDSA P-256 challenge-response.
stepUp.publicKeyHelper: ECDSA P-256 public key in SPKI format, Base64 encoded
stepUp.keyGenFailed: Failed to generate key pair. You can paste a key manually.
stepUp.challengePrompt: Request a challenge to verify your device registration.
stepUp.challengeLabel: Challenge (sign this with your private key):
stepUp.signedChallengeHelper: Sign the challenge above with your private key and paste the Base64-encoded signature
stepUp.completeDescription: Your device has been registered for step-up authentication. It can now be used for sensitive operations.

auth.forgotPasswordSubtitle: Enter your email and we'll send you a reset link
auth.forgotPasswordSuccess: If that email exists, a reset link has been sent.
auth.resetPasswordSuccess: Your password has been reset successfully. You can now sign in with your new password.
auth.resetPasswordError: Failed to reset password. The link may have expired.
auth.resetPasswordInvalidToken: Invalid or missing reset token. Please request a new reset link.
auth.noAccount: Don't have an account? Sign up
auth.hasAccount: Already have an account? Sign in

nfc.title: NFC Enrollment
nfc.subtitle: Scan an NFC card to enroll, verify, or search for its owner.
nfc.unsupportedDescription: Web NFC is only supported on Chrome for Android. To enroll or manage NFC cards, please use the FIVUCSAS mobile app.
nfc.unsupportedHint: Tip: Open this page on Chrome for Android with NFC enabled to use browser-based NFC enrollment.
nfc.waiting: Hold your NFC card near the device...
nfc.noOwner: No user found for this card.

faceSearch.title: Face Search
faceSearch.subtitle: Capture a face to search the enrolled database. The system will identify matching users using 1:N face recognition.
faceSearch.noMatching: No matching faces found in the enrolled database.

voiceSearch.title: Voice Search
voiceSearch.subtitle: Record your voice to search the enrolled speaker database. The system will identify matching users using 1:N voice recognition.
voiceSearch.noMatching: No matching voices found in the enrolled database.
voiceSearch.microphoneError: Unable to access microphone. Please grant permissions.

cardDetection.title: Card Detection
cardDetection.subtitle: Capture an image of an ID card, passport, or driver's license. The server-side YOLO model will detect and classify the card type.

authFlows.title: Authentication Flows
authFlows.subtitle: Configure multi-step authentication sequences per operation type
authFlows.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the flow "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
authFlows.noFlowsHint: Create your first flow to get started
authFlows.noStepsYet: No steps added yet. Click "Add Step" to start building your authentication flow.

guests.title: Guest Management
guests.subtitle: Invite and manage temporary guest access
guests.noGuestsHint: Click "Invite Guest" to send a temporary access invitation
guests.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this guest's access? This action cannot be undone.

developerPortal.title: Developer Portal
developerPortal.subtitle: Register applications and manage OAuth 2.0 client credentials.
developerPortal.redirectUrisHelper: Comma-separated list of allowed redirect URIs
developerPortal.noAppsHint: Click "Register New App" to create your first OAuth 2.0 client.
developerPortal.secretWarning: Copy the client secret now. It will not be shown again for security reasons.
developerPortal.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This will revoke all active tokens immediately.
developerPortal.deleteFailed: Failed to delete application. Please try again.
developerPortal.registerFailed: Failed to register application. Please try again.
developerPortal.backendPending: Backend integration pending — data is stored in browser memory for demonstration purposes only.
developerPortal.quickStartDesc: Integrate FIVUCSAS authentication into your application in three simple steps.
developerPortal.step1Desc: Click "Register New App" above to get your client_id and client_secret. Configure your allowed redirect URIs and scopes.
developerPortal.step2Desc: Include the FIVUCSAS auth widget in your HTML page:
developerPortal.step3Desc: Exchange the authorization code for tokens on your callback page:

widgetDemo.title: Auth Widget
widgetDemo.description: The FIVUCSAS embeddable authentication widget -- like Stripe Elements, but for biometric identity verification. Drop a single script tag into any website to add multi-factor biometric auth.
widgetDemo.liveDemoDesc: Try the widget in real time. The SDK communicates with the verify-app via postMessage and renders inside an isolated iframe.
widgetDemo.tryModalHint: Opens verification in a centered overlay
widgetDemo.tryInlineHint: Embeds verification directly in the page below
widgetDemo.architectureDesc: Three-layer architecture isolating sensitive biometric capture inside a sandboxed iframe. The host page never touches raw biometric data.
widgetDemo.layer2Subtitle: Manages auth flow steps inside the iframe
widgetDemo.layer3Subtitle: Biometric data stays within the sandboxed origin
widgetDemo.htmlDesc: The simplest integration. Drop the script tag and Web Component into any HTML page. No build tools required -- just a complete working page.
widgetDemo.jsDesc: Full programmatic control. Use the FivucsasAuth class directly for modal or inline embedding with promise-based completion.
widgetDemo.reactDesc: Complete React component with TypeScript types, error handling, and inline container support. This is the same pattern the FIVUCSAS dashboard uses internally.
widgetDemo.footer: FIVUCSAS dogfooding -- the platform consuming its own auth widget
